novel_features.name: Cross-store price comparison
novel_features.description: Compare item pricing across every nearby store and find the cheapest one for your order.
novel_features.rationale: Requires syncing menu and pricing data from multiple stores into a local SQLite database, then joining against a candidate cart. Community wrappers only query one store at a time.
novel_features.example: dominos-pp-cli compare-prices --address "421 N 63rd St, Seattle WA" --items S_PIZPH,S_LAVA --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pizza prices vary by store. Reach for this when the user cares about saving money or has a flexible delivery radius.

novel_features.name: Named order templates
novel_features.description: Save a complete order (store, address, items, toppings, payment) as a named template and replay it with one command.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite-stored named templates that survive across sessions. apizza has cart persistence but no named templates; node-dominos has no persistence at all.
novel_features.example: dominos-pp-cli template save "friday-night" --from-cart && dominos-pp-cli template order "friday-night"
novel_features.why_it_matters: Most users order the same thing repeatedly. Reach for this when the user says 'usual order' or 'what I had last Friday'.

novel_features.name: Deal optimizer
novel_features.description: Cross-references the cart against every available deal (including loyalty-exclusive) to find the cheapest combination.
novel_features.rationale: Domino's CheckDraftDeal endpoint only validates one deal at a time. Optimizing across N deals requires running each candidate locally and comparing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The headline price often hides a cheaper deal-applied path. Reach for this whenever the cart total is non-trivial.

novel_features.name: Menu diff
novel_features.description: Compare the current menu against the last-synced snapshot to surface new items, removed items, and price changes.
novel_features.rationale: Requires preserving a historical menu snapshot in SQLite. Domino's API only returns current state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Power users want to know when menu items appear or change price. Reach for this for periodic health checks of a favorite store.

novel_features.name: Spending analytics
novel_features.description: Aggregate order history into spending trends, favorite items, order frequency, and average order value over a chosen window.
novel_features.rationale: Order history endpoints are auth-gated and only return raw data. SQLite-backed aggregation produces analytics no server-side query exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Useful for budgeting and 'how much do I actually spend on pizza?' questions. Reach for this when the user asks for trends.

novel_features.name: Live delivery tracker with polling
novel_features.description: Polls the tracker endpoint at a chosen interval and streams status updates: prep → bake → quality check → out for delivery.
novel_features.rationale: node-dominos exposes a single tracker call. A watch loop with structured status transitions and an exit-on-delivered signal is a CLI-shaped feature.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents and users alike want a non-blocking 'tell me when the pizza arrives' primitive. Reach for this immediately after order place.

novel_features.name: Smart reorder with substitution
novel_features.description: Replay your last order against today's menu, automatically substituting unavailable items with the closest match using FTS similarity.
novel_features.rationale: Requires both order history and the full current menu in the local store. Existing wrappers have neither.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Menus change. The 'usual' order may no longer be valid verbatim but can be reproduced in spirit. Reach for this when reorder fails strict.

novel_features.name: Nutrition calculator
novel_features.description: Sum calories, protein, fat, and carbs across all items in a cart using the menu's embedded nutrition data.
novel_features.rationale: Domino's menu includes nutrition data per item. Aggregating across a cart requires local persistence; no existing tool does this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Health-conscious users want to know cart-level totals before ordering. Reach for this when nutrition is mentioned.

novel_features.name: Bulk order builder
novel_features.description: Read a CSV of multi-person orders, find the optimal store for the group, and submit each individual order.
novel_features.rationale: No existing tool supports multi-order batch. Useful for office lunches and parties where one coordinator is buying for many.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Group ordering is painful. Reach for this when the user has more than 3 individual orders to place.

novel_features.name: Store health score
novel_features.description: Composite store health score combining wait times, hours, service capabilities, and historical delivery performance.
novel_features.rationale: CartEtaMinutes + store profile + capability flags need to be joined into a single rating. No tool does this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Two stores at similar distances can have very different ETAs. Reach for this when the user is choosing between options.

narrative.headline: Order pizza, browse menus, optimize deals, and track delivery from the terminal — with a local SQLite store that powers reorder, analytics, and price comparison no other Domino's tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: Every Domino's feature you would expect — store locator, menu browse, build cart, validate, price, place, and track — plus a local data layer that compounds. Save named templates with `template save`, find the cheapest store for your order with `compare-prices`, hunt for the best deal with `deals best`, and watch your delivery in real-time with `track --watch`.
narrative.auth_narrative: Most commands work without authentication: store locator, menu browse, cart building, anonymous order placement, and tracking by phone all succeed unauthenticated. For loyalty rewards, member-exclusive deals, and order history, run `dominos-pp-cli auth login` to obtain an OAuth token from authproxy.dominos.com — the token is cached in `~/.config/dominos-pp-cli/`. Power users can also export `DOMINOS_TOKEN` directly.

quickstart.command: dominos-pp-cli stores find --address "421 N 63rd St" --city "Seattle WA"

quickstart.comment: Pull the menu into the local SQLite database
Offline FTS5 search across menu items, toppings, and descriptions

quickstart.command: dominos-pp-cli cart add S_PIZPH --size large --topping pepperoni:full:2
quickstart.comment: Build the order with intuitive topping syntax
Preview the full request before sending; remove --dry-run to actually order

troubleshoots.fix: Domino's geocoder is strict; pass street and city/state separately: `--address "421 N 63rd St" --city "Seattle WA"`
Domino's blocks non-US IPs. Check `dominos-pp-cli doctor` for region detection. Use a US-located machine or VPN.
Run `dominos-pp-cli auth login --debug` to see the exact authproxy response. Most often a typo in email/password or 2FA challenge.

troubleshoots.symptom: Track command shows order not found
troubleshoots.fix: Use the phone number tied to the order, not your account phone. The tracker is keyed on the order phone field.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or power user needs to interact with Domino's outside a browser — building, pricing, and placing orders, tracking deliveries, comparing prices across stores, optimizing deal selection, or analyzing past spending. Excellent for automation: every command supports --json, --dry-run, --agent, and structured exit codes. Local SQLite store enables features the public API cannot serve directly (reorder substitution, menu diff, deal optimization, spending trends).

recipes.title: Friday night reorder
recipes.explanation: Replay a saved template, place the order, and tail the tracker until delivery.

recipes.title: Find cheapest store for tonight's order
recipes.explanation: Surveys every nearby store, computes the same cart at each, and reports the cheapest.

recipes.title: Hunt for the best deal
recipes.explanation: Tries every available deal against the current cart and reports the lowest total with the deal code.

recipes.title: Watch a delivery in real-time
recipes.explanation: Streams status transitions every 30 seconds until the order is delivered.

recipes.title: How much did I spend on pizza this quarter
recipes.explanation: Aggregates synced order history into spending totals, frequency, and favorite items.

trigger_phrases: how much do i spend on pizza
